title: Understanding Anxiety: What It Is and What It Isn't
titleAr: فهم القلق: ما هو وما ليس كذلك
learningObjective: After this exercise, the user will be able to distinguish between normal anxiety and anxiety disorders, identify 3 common misconceptions, and recognize when professional help may be beneficial.
learningObjectiveAr: بعد هذا التمرين، سيتمكن المستخدم من التمييز بين القلق الطبيعي واضطرابات القلق.

content.scenario:
Anxiety is one of the most misunderstood terms in mental health.

**What anxiety IS:**
- A normal emotional response to perceived threats (Jorm, 2012)
- Present in everyone at some level
- Can be helpful (alerts us to real dangers)

**What anxiety is NOT:**
- A choice or personality weakness
- The same as being 'just worried'
- Something you can 'just get over'

**When it may indicate a disorder (educational, NOT diagnostic):**
- Persistent (most days for 6+ months)
- Disproportionate to the actual threat
- Significantly impairs daily functioning (work, relationships, sleep)
- Physical symptoms: racing heart, difficulty breathing, muscle tension

⚠️ DISCLAIMER: This is EDUCATIONAL content. Only qualified professionals can diagnose anxiety disorders.
content.scenarioAr: القلق من أكثر المصطلحات سوء فهم في الصحة النفسية.

task.instructions: For each statement, identify whether it is a FACT or a MYTH about anxiety.
task.instructionsAr: لكل عبارة، حدد ما إذا كانت حقيقة أم خرافة عن القلق.

items.explanation: FACT. Anxiety is a universal human experience. It becomes a concern when it's persistent, disproportionate, and impairing.

items.text: 'If you just relax, your anxiety will go away.' → MYTH
items.explanation: MYTH. Telling someone to 'just relax' misunderstands anxiety. Anxiety disorders involve neurobiological mechanisms that cannot be simply 'thought away.'

items.text: 'Anxiety disorders are very common among university students.' → FACT
items.explanation: FACT. Research consistently shows anxiety is one of the most prevalent mental health concerns among university populations worldwide.

feedback.correct: Well done! You can now distinguish facts from myths about anxiety. This knowledge helps reduce stigma and encourages appropriate help-seeking.
feedback.correctAr: أحسنت! يمكنك الآن التمييز بين الحقائق والخرافات عن القلق.
feedback.incorrect: Review each statement carefully. Myths often contain a tiny grain of truth wrapped in a harmful misunderstanding.

whatNotToDo: Don't diagnose yourself or others based on this exercise — it's educational only
Don't dismiss anxiety as 'not real' — it has neurobiological underpinnings
evidence: Jorm, A.F. (2012). Mental health literacy. American Psychologist, 67(3), 231-243.
safetyNote: This is educational content, NOT diagnostic. For clinical assessment, consult a qualified mental health professional. Egypt crisis line: 16328.
com_b_mechanism: Distinguishing anxiety facts from myths builds recognition capability
